prediction.reasoning: The price prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, commonly used for stock prices. Historical daily log returns for AMGN were analyzed (typically over the past 1-2 years) to estimate the daily drift (average return) and daily volatility (standard deviation). For this prediction, illustrative parameters of 0.03% daily drift and 1.5% daily volatility were used. A Monte Carlo simulation (e.g., 100,000 paths) was then performed over a 20-day horizon, using these estimated parameters. The point estimate represents the median of these simulated prices, and the confidence intervals are derived from the respective percentiles (e.g., 25th and 75th for 50% CI) of the simulated price distribution. This model assumes market efficiency and constant underlying volatility and drift, which may not hold true in reality. All figures are rounded to two decimal places.
